stem: Two investments are made: Account X earns simple interest at an annual rate of 6%, and Account Y earns interest compounded once per year at an annual rate of 6%. Both accounts begin with the same initial deposit and no further deposits or withdrawals are made. Which of the following best describes the annual interest earned by the two accounts?

answerOptions.content: In every year after the first year, Account X earns less interest than Account Y.
Account X always earns more interest per year than Account Y.
Account X earns the same amount of interest every year as Account Y.
Account X earns more interest per year than Account Y at first but eventually earns less.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. In year 1, both accounts earn the same amount: 6% of the initial deposit. Starting in year 2, Account Y's balance has grown because the year-1 interest is added to the principal that earns interest the next year, so its annual interest exceeds 6% of the original principal. Account X, in contrast, continues to earn exactly 6% of the original principal each year. Therefore, in every year after the first, Account X earns less interest than Account Y.

Choice B is incorrect because Account X never earns more than Account Y after year 1 (and the two are equal in year 1). Choice C is incorrect: only the first year's interest is equal. Choice D reverses the relationship — simple interest never overtakes compound at the same rate.
